title: What Is a SETA in South Africa? Complete Guide 2026
intro: If you've been researching learnerships or skills training in South Africa, you've probably come across the word SETA. In this guide, we explain exactly what a SETA is, how it works, and how you can use SETAs to access free training and learnerships — in plain language.

body_sections.heading: What Does SETA Stand For?
body_sections.content: SETA stands for Sector Education and Training Authority. There are 21 SETAs in South Africa, each responsible for a different sector of the economy — from construction to banking to agriculture.

body_sections.heading: What Does a SETA Do?
body_sections.content: SETAs are funded by a 1% levy that companies pay on their payroll. This money goes into a Skills Development Fund. SETAs use this fund to pay companies to train unemployed people through learnerships, apprenticeships, and skills programmes.

body_sections.heading: How Do SETAs Help Job Seekers?
body_sections.content: When a company runs a learnership funded by a SETA, they take on unemployed people as learners. The learner receives on-the-job training AND a nationally recognised qualification — all free of charge, with a monthly stipend.

faq.q: How many SETAs are there in South Africa?
faq.a: There are 21 SETAs covering different sectors of the South African economy.

faq.q: Do I need to contact a SETA directly to get a learnership?
faq.a: No. You apply to companies that offer SETA-funded learnerships, not to the SETA itself.
Yes. All legitimate SETA-funded learnerships are completely free. Any programme that charges an application or registration fee is NOT a legitimate SETA learnership.

meta_title: What Is a SETA in South Africa? Complete 2026 Guide
meta_description: Learn what a SETA is, how SETAs fund learnerships, and how to access SETA opportunities in South Africa. Clear, plain-language guide updated for 2026.

title: What Is a Learnership in South Africa? 2026 Explained
intro: A learnership is one of the most valuable opportunities for unemployed South Africans — it pays you a monthly stipend while you earn a nationally recognised qualification. Here's everything you need to know.

body_sections.heading: The Structure of a Learnership
body_sections.content: A learnership consists of two parts: theoretical learning (usually at a college or training center) and practical workplace experience. This means you get to study and work at the same time.

body_sections.heading: Duration & Stipends
body_sections.content: Most learnerships run for 12 months. While you are studying and working, the employer will pay you a monthly stipend to cover your basic travel and living costs. For matriculants, stipends typically range from R3,000 to R6,000.

faq.q: How long does a learnership last?
faq.a: Most learnerships run for 12 months, though some technical learnerships can last up to 24 months.
A stipend is a monthly allowance paid to learners. In 2026, most learnerships pay between R3 500 and R6 000 per month.

faq.q: Can I do a learnership if I already have a job?
faq.a: Most learnerships are for unemployed people. Employed learnerships exist but are less common — check each opportunity's eligibility carefully.

meta_title: What Is a Learnership? South Africa Guide 2026
meta_description: A learnership pays you a monthly stipend while you earn an NQF qualification. Find out how learnerships work, who can apply, and how to get one in South Africa.

title: How to Apply for Bursaries in South Africa: Complete Guide
intro: Applying for a bursary is your ticket to a debt-free education. But the application process is rigorous, and missing a single document can result in instant disqualification. Here is your step-by-step guide to applying for bursaries in 2026.

body_sections.heading: Step 1: Check Your Eligibility
body_sections.content: Before you begin, read the minimum requirements carefully. Don't waste time applying for an Engineering bursary if you have Maths Literacy. Ensure your household income meets their financial need criteria.

body_sections.heading: Step 2: Collect Certified Documents
body_sections.content: You will need a certified ID, proof of residence, proof of parent/guardian income, and your latest academic transcript. Go to the police station and get multiple copies certified so you're always ready.

body_sections.heading: Step 3: The Motivation Letter
body_sections.content: Write a strong motivation letter that tells your story. Explain why you deserve the funding, what your career goals are, and how the bursary will change your life. Do not copy and paste generic templates.

faq.q: Do I have to pay back a bursary?
faq.a: Unlike student loans, bursaries generally do not have to be paid back, provided you pass your modules. However, some corporate bursaries require you to work for the company for a few years after graduation.
Most corporate bursaries open between April and August for the following academic year. NSFAS generally opens in September.

faq.q: Can I apply for multiple bursaries?
faq.a: Yes! You should apply for as many bursaries as you qualify for to increase your chances.

meta_title: How to Apply for Bursaries in South Africa (2026 Guidelines)
meta_description: A comprehensive guide on how to apply for bursaries in South Africa. Covering documents, motivation letters, deadlines, and eligibility criteria.

title: Documents Needed for Learnerships in South Africa
intro: Having the correct documents ready is the secret to applying quickly and successfully. Here is the ultimate checklist of documents you need to prepare for learnership applications in 2026.

body_sections.heading: The Big Three
body_sections.content: Almost every learnership will ask for these three documents: A certified copy of your South African ID, a certified copy of your Matric certificate (or highest qualification), and your comprehensive CV.

body_sections.heading: Certification Rules
body_sections.content: Never send your original documents. You must submit certified copies. Go to a police station, post office, or bank with the originals and copies. The certification stamp must not be older than 3 months.

body_sections.heading: Proof of Residence & Affidavits
body_sections.content: Many companies give preference to candidates from the local community, so they will ask for a proof of residence. If you are unemployed, they may also ask for an affidavit stating your unemployment status.

faq.q: Can I use an uncertified ID copy?
faq.a: No, uncertified copies are almost always rejected immediately by HR departments.

faq.q: How long should my CV be?
faq.a: If you are a matriculant with no experience, your CV should be a maximum of 1-2 pages.

faq.q: What if I lost my Matric certificate?
faq.a: You must apply for a replacement certificate at the Department of Basic Education, which can take several weeks.

meta_title: Required Documents for Learnerships in SA (Checklist 2026)
meta_description: Don't get rejected for missing documents. Use our definitive checklist of certified documents and requirements for South African learnerships.

title: Spotting Fake Jobs: How to Avoid Learnership Scams
intro: Scammers target desperate job seekers with fake learnerships and jobs. If you know what to look for, you can protect yourself. Here is how to verify an opportunity and avoid being scammed in South Africa.

body_sections.heading: Red Flag 1: They Ask for Money
body_sections.content: This is the biggest warning sign. Legitimate learnerships are 100% free to apply for. If someone asks for an 'administration fee', 'uniform fee', or 'background check fee', it is a scam. Walk away.

body_sections.heading: Red Flag 2: Suspicious Email Addresses
body_sections.content: Official companies do not use @gmail.com or @yahoo.com addresses for recruitment. If an 'Eskom' recruiter emails you from 'eskom.recruitment123@gmail.com', it is a scam.

body_sections.heading: Red Flag 3: Unprofessional Communication
body_sections.content: Legitimate employers will not conduct a hiring process entirely through WhatsApp without a formal interview process or official company documentation.

faq.q: Is it safe to send my ID copy via WhatsApp?
faq.a: No. Never send your ID copy or personal documents to unknown numbers on WhatsApp, as identity theft is a major risk.

faq.q: How can I check if a company is real?
faq.a: You can verify the company registration number on the CIPC website, and always search for the company's official career portal.

faq.q: What should I do if I find a scam?
faq.a: Report the scam to the South African Police Service (SAPS) and warn others on social media.

meta_title: How to Avoid Learnership & Job Scams in South Africa
meta_description: Protect your identity and your money. Learn the top red flags of fake learnerships and how to verify if a job opportunity in SA is legitimate.

title: NQF Levels in South Africa Explained
intro: When you look at learnerships or qualifications, you will see 'NQF Level 4' or 'NQF Level 6'. The National Qualifications Framework (NQF) is South Africa's system for organising education. Here is what those levels actually mean for your career.

body_sections.heading: What is the NQF?
body_sections.content: The NQF is a 10-level system used to measure and compare qualifications. It ensures that a qualification from a college, a university, or a learnership holds a standard verified value.

body_sections.heading: Level 1 to 4: The Schooling Levels
body_sections.content: NQF Level 1 is a Grade 9 pass. NQF Level 4 is the most important milestone—this is a Grade 12 / Matric certificate. Most learnerships require you to have an NQF Level 4 before you can apply.

body_sections.heading: Level 5 to 7: Diplomas and Degrees
body_sections.content: NQF Level 5 is a Higher Certificate. NQF Level 6 is a National Diploma (often completed at a TVET or University of Technology). NQF Level 7 represents a Bachelor's Degree.

faq.q: Can I do a learnership if I don't have NQF Level 4 (Matric)?
faq.a: Yes, but options are limited. There are NQF Level 2 and 3 learnerships (often in trades or cleaning), but NQF Level 4 significantly opens up your opportunities.

faq.q: Is a learnership certificate the same as a university degree?
faq.a: It depends on the NQF Level. An NQF Level 6 diploma from a learnership has the same NQF weight as an NQF 6 diploma from a university, though the focus is professional rather than academic.

meta_title: NQF Levels South Africa Explained (Levels 1 to 10)
meta_description: Confused about NQF levels? Learn what NQF Level 4, 5, 6, and 7 mean, and how they apply to Matric, learnerships, diplomas, and degrees in South Africa.
